description: Turn Clicky from a server-proxied app into a shippable personal tool: each user installs the binary, opens a settings window on first launch, pastes their own API keys for Anthropic and/or z.ai (LLM) plus AssemblyAI (streaming transcription) and ElevenLabs (TTS), and Clicky talks directly to those providers from the user's machine. No Cloudflare Worker. No shared backend. No keys on disk in plaintext — all secrets encrypted with Windows DPAPI under the current user's profile. The existing worker/ directory remains in the repo because it is shared with the Mac reference implementation, but the Windows client no longer calls it in any code path. Users can switch between Claude and GLM at runtime via a tray menu without restarting. This PRD builds on the completed Windows port (see archive/prd-windows-port.json for the 17 shipped stories) and supersedes the earlier worker-routing GLM integration draft (see archive/prd-glm-worker-routing.json) that this project decided against because it required every user to run Cloudflare Wrangler locally. Target vision model for GLM: glm-4.6v. The Mac reference implementation is not modified.

userStories.title: ILlmClient interface + AnthropicDirectClient (direct to api.anthropic.com)
userStories.description: As a user with my own Anthropic API key, I want Clicky to call api.anthropic.com directly with my key instead of going through a worker, so I can run the app without any backend infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: New interface `Clicky.Api.ILlmClient` exposes `IAsyncEnumerable<string> SendAsync(IReadOnlyList<Message> history, IReadOnlyList<CapturedScreen> screens, string systemPrompt, string userText, CancellationToken ct)` — the exact surface `CompanionManager` already consumes, extracted from the existing `ClaudeClient`

The existing `Clicky.Api.ClaudeClient` is replaced by `Clicky.Api.AnthropicDirectClient : ILlmClient` that POSTs to `https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages` directly with headers `x-api-key: <userKey>`, `anthropic-version: 2023-06-01`, `content-type: application/json`
Constructor signature: `AnthropicDirectClient(string apiKey, string model = "claude-sonnet-4-6", HttpClient? httpClient = null)`. No proxy URL — the worker path is gone.

Request body shape, SSE parsing, PNG/JPEG magic-byte media_type detection, and TLS warmup HEAD request behavior are preserved byte-for-byte from the old ClaudeClient (see US-007 notes in archive/progress-windows-port.txt)

`Clicky.Companion.CompanionManager` now takes `ILlmClient` via constructor injection instead of constructing a `ClaudeClient` itself. App.xaml.cs constructs `AnthropicDirectClient` with a temporary hardcoded key for this story only — real key plumbing lands in US-019
All existing US-007 tests (SSE parsing, delta extraction, media type detection) pass against the new AnthropicDirectClient unchanged
One new test: a `FakeLlmClient : ILlmClient` verifies CompanionManager's dependency injection seam works end-to-end
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all existing Windows-port tests still pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-018 - ILlmClient interface + AnthropicDirectClient
userStories.notes: This is primarily a refactor + direct-API swap. Keep the scope narrow: don't add provider selection, key storage, or UI yet — those land in US-019 through US-024. The hardcoded temporary key is fine for this story because US-019 will replace it within one iteration.

userStories.title: SecretsStore (DPAPI) + SettingsStore (JSON) under %APPDATA%\Clicky
userStories.description: As a user, I want my API keys encrypted at rest using my Windows account credentials, and my non-secret settings persisted in a readable JSON file, so nobody can extract my keys from disk and I can inspect/reset my config without a GUI if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: `Clicky.Companion.SecretsStore` wraps `System.Security.Cryptography.ProtectedData.Protect` / `Unprotect` with `DataProtectionScope.CurrentUser`. Methods: `void Write(string key, string value)`, `string? Read(string key)`, `void Delete(string key)`, `bool Exists(string key)`
Secrets are stored as an encrypted blob at `%APPDATA%\Clicky\secrets.bin`. The plaintext internal format is a simple JSON dictionary; encryption is applied to the whole file, not per-entry

`Clicky.Companion.SettingsStore` reads/writes `%APPDATA%\Clicky\settings.json` with fields: `LlmProvider` (string: "anthropic" or "zai"), `LlmModel` (string), `ElevenLabsVoiceId` (string, default `kPzsL2i3teMYv0FxEYQ6` to match Mac), `OnboardingComplete` (bool), `AnalyticsOptOut` (bool)
Both stores auto-create the %APPDATA%\Clicky directory on first write
Keys used by SecretsStore are standardized constants: `anthropic_api_key`, `zai_api_key`, `assemblyai_api_key`, `elevenlabs_api_key`

App.xaml.cs on OnStartup instantiates SettingsStore + SecretsStore, reads the configured provider/model/keys, and passes them into the CompanionManager constructor (replacing the hardcoded key from US-018)

US-014's OnboardingService registry read is migrated: if `HKCU\Software\Clicky\onboarded` exists, copy its value to SettingsStore.OnboardingComplete on first run of this story's code, then prefer the SettingsStore value thereafter. Same for `analyticsOptOut`

Unit tests: SecretsStore round-trip (write → read → same value), SecretsStore decryption under a different user profile fails gracefully (returns null rather than throwing), SettingsStore round-trip, SettingsStore defaults when file is missing, migration from registry test
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass on a Windows test host
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-019 - SecretsStore (DPAPI) + SettingsStore under APPDATA
userStories.notes: DPAPI is per-user-account, not per-machine — backups restored to a different Windows user will fail to decrypt, which is correct security behavior. Document this in the SettingsWindow help text (lands in US-022). Do NOT add a passphrase fallback; the whole point of DPAPI is that it's invisible to the user.

userStories.title: ZaiDirectClient for GLM models (direct to api.z.ai OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
userStories.description: As a user with a z.ai API key, I want Clicky to use GLM-4.6V as a vision-capable alternative to Claude, with the same UI and screen-pointing behavior, so I have a choice of LLM providers.
acceptanceCriteria: New `Clicky.Api.ZaiDirectClient : ILlmClient` POSTs to `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/chat/completions` with headers `Authorization: Bearer <userKey>`, `Content-Type: application/json`, `Accept: text/event-stream`
Constructor: `ZaiDirectClient(string apiKey, string model = "glm-4.6v", HttpClient? httpClient = null)`

Translates Clicky's existing Anthropic-shape inputs (system prompt + message history + CapturedScreen list + user text) into OpenAI chat-completions request body: system prompt becomes `messages[0] = {role:"system", content: systemPrompt}`, history turns pass through with their existing roles, the final user turn's text + screens become one user message with a `content` array of `{type:"text", text:...}` and `{type:"image_url", image_url:{url:"data:<media_type>;base64,<base64Data>"}}` entries
Image media_type is sniffed by PNG/JPEG magic bytes (reuse `AnthropicDirectClient.DetectImageMediaType` via refactor or duplicate — do not import a new library)

Streaming response parsing: reads `data: {...}` SSE lines, extracts `choices[0].delta.content` text chunks, yields them through the `IAsyncEnumerable<string>` return (same interface shape as AnthropicDirectClient). The `[DONE]` sentinel ends the stream cleanly
Chunk-boundary UTF-8 splits (a multi-byte character split across two HTTP chunks) do not corrupt the yielded text — use an incremental decoder, not a per-chunk Encoding.UTF8.GetString
Malformed JSON lines are logged and skipped, not thrown — one bad line must not kill the stream

Valid model values accepted: `glm-4.6v` (default, vision), `glm-4.5v` (fallback, vision). Text-only models (`glm-5.1`, `glm-5-turbo`, `glm-5`, `glm-4.5-air`, `glm-4.7-no-vision-variants`) must NOT be offered as choices because they silently break the [POINT:x,y:label:screenN] feature — Clicky requires vision

Unit tests: text-only request translation, single-image request translation, multi-image multi-screen request translation, SSE chunk parsing fixture (realistic glm-4.6v response with a [POINT:...] tag mid-stream), UTF-8 chunk-boundary split test, malformed-chunk skip test
End-to-end test with FakeHttpMessageHandler: canned OpenAI SSE input → expected string deltas out through ILlmClient.SendAsync
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-020 - ZaiDirectClient for GLM-4.6V
userStories.notes: This is the most algorithmically dense story. Budget time for the SSE chunk-boundary tests — getting them right is what separates a demo-quality integration from a shippable one. The Anthropic-shape system prompt (built by CompanionManager in US-010, includes the [POINT:x,y:label:screenN] tag instructions) passes through unchanged — GLM-4.6V is prompted in the same English, it just speaks OpenAI over the wire.

userStories.title: Direct-to-provider audio clients (AssemblyAI + ElevenLabs, no worker)
userStories.description: As a user with AssemblyAI and ElevenLabs API keys, I want Clicky to call those services directly with my keys, so I can run the app without any Cloudflare Worker.
acceptanceCriteria: `Clicky.Api.AssemblyAiStreamingTranscriber` is updated: the existing `FetchTokenAsync` that hits `{workerBaseUrl}/transcribe-token` is replaced with a direct call to `https://streaming.assemblyai.com/v3/token?expires_in_seconds=60` with header `Authorization: <userKey>` (AssemblyAI's token endpoint uses the raw API key, NOT `Bearer`)
Constructor: `AssemblyAiStreamingTranscriber(string apiKey, HttpClient? httpClient = null)` — proxyUrl parameter removed

The WebSocket connection to `wss://streaming.assemblyai.com/v3/ws` and all session mechanics (keyterms, sample_rate=16000, encoding=pcm_s16le, format_turns=true, speech_model=u3-rt-pro, partial/final turn processing, error callback, ForceEndpoint grace period) are preserved from US-008 unchanged

`Clicky.Api.ElevenLabsTtsClient` is updated: existing POST to `{workerBaseUrl}/tts` is replaced with a direct POST to `https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/text-to-speech/{voiceId}/stream` with headers `xi-api-key: <userKey>`, `Content-Type: application/json`, `Accept: audio/mpeg` and body `{text, model_id: "eleven_flash_v2_5", voice_settings: {stability: 0.5, similarity_boost: 0.75}}`

Constructor: `ElevenLabsTtsClient(string apiKey, string voiceId, HttpClient? httpClient = null)`. The voice ID is now a constructor parameter (read from SettingsStore.ElevenLabsVoiceId by App.xaml.cs)
MP3 decoding via NAudio Mp3FileReader and WaveOutEvent playback path from US-009 is preserved unchanged

All existing US-008 and US-009 unit tests continue to pass after the refactor (session URI builder, keyterm serialization, partial/final dispatch, IsPlaying, StopPlayback, Dispose safety, cancellation)
New tests: direct token fetch URL + header format, direct TTS POST URL + header format, voice ID parameter threading
App.xaml.cs constructs both clients with keys + voice ID from SecretsStore + SettingsStore; CompanionManager receives them via constructor injection (matching the pattern from US-018/US-019)
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-021 - Direct AssemblyAI and ElevenLabs clients
userStories.notes: AssemblyAI's token-fetch auth uses the raw key in the Authorization header, not `Bearer <key>` — double-check this before coding. This is the last story that touches the Clicky.Api layer; after it, the worker is completely unused by the Windows client.

userStories.title: SettingsWindow WPF UI with Test buttons
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want a friendly settings window where I can paste my API keys, pick my LLM provider and model, and verify each key works with a Test button before saving.
acceptanceCriteria: New `Clicky.App.SettingsWindow.xaml` — resizable, non-modal, dark theme matching CompanionPanelWindow, ~480×640 DIP default size, centered on primary display

Fields (top to bottom): **LLM Provider** (ComboBox: `Anthropic Claude`, `z.ai GLM`), **LLM Model** (ComboBox whose items change based on provider: Anthropic → `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`, `claude-opus-4-6`; z.ai → `glm-4.6v`, `glm-4.5v`), **Anthropic API Key** (PasswordBox + Test button + `Get key →` link), **z.ai API Key** (PasswordBox + Test button + link), **AssemblyAI API Key** (PasswordBox + Test button + link), **ElevenLabs API Key** (PasswordBox + Test button + link), **ElevenLabs Voice ID** (TextBox, pre-filled with `kPzsL2i3teMYv0FxEYQ6`)

`Get key →` links open the default browser to: Anthropic → `https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys`, z.ai → `https://z.ai/manage-apikey/apikey-list`, AssemblyAI → `https://www.assemblyai.com/app/account`, ElevenLabs → `https://elevenlabs.io/app/settings/api-keys`

Each Test button fires a one-shot auth probe against the corresponding service using the currently-entered key (not the saved one), shows a spinner during the probe, and then turns green (✓) or red (✗) with a tooltip containing the error message. Probes: Anthropic → POST /v1/messages with `max_tokens: 1, model: claude-haiku-4-5, messages: [{role: user, content: "hi"}]`; z.ai → POST /api/paas/v4/chat/completions with `max_tokens: 1, model: glm-4.5v, messages: [{role: user, content: "hi"}]`; AssemblyAI → GET /v3/token?expires_in_seconds=60 (returns token on success, 401 on bad key); ElevenLabs → GET /v1/user/subscription (free auth check, returns subscription info)

Save button writes keys via SecretsStore, non-secret fields via SettingsStore, raises a `SettingsSaved` event, and closes the window. Save is enabled only when the required keys for the selected provider are entered (Anthropic provider → Anthropic key required; z.ai provider → z.ai key required; AssemblyAI + ElevenLabs keys always required because audio is mandatory)
Quit button (secondary, less prominent) calls Application.Current.Shutdown — required for the first-run case where the user doesn't want to enter keys
Help text at the bottom: small italic footnote explaining "Your keys are encrypted with your Windows account and stored only on this PC. Clicky never uploads them anywhere."
Window is reachable from the existing tray right-click menu via a new `Settings...` item (added between `Open` and `Quit`)
When reopened after initial save, fields are pre-populated from SecretsStore/SettingsStore (passwords shown as placeholder `••••••••` with a `Change` button that clears the field)
Unit tests: ViewModel command enablement (Save disabled until required fields entered), model list updates when provider changes, SecretsStore persistence on Save
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass. Manual: open Settings from tray, fill fields, all four Test buttons work against real services with a real key
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-022 - SettingsWindow UI with Test buttons
userStories.notes: Copy matters here — this is the onboarding experience for everyone who installs Clicky. Use friendly, plain English. Avoid words like 'provider', 'endpoint', 'API key rejected (401)'. Prefer 'Service', 'Couldn't reach Anthropic — check your key'. Do not add import/export or encryption passphrase features in this story; scope-creep risk is high.

userStories.title: First-run setup flow
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want Clicky to walk me through entering my API keys the first time I launch it, and to re-open the settings window on subsequent launches if I'm missing a required key, so I never hit a confusing silent failure.
acceptanceCriteria: On App.OnStartup, after the existing SettingsStore + SecretsStore initialization (US-019), check: (a) are the currently-selected provider's LLM key and the two audio keys (AssemblyAI + ElevenLabs) all present in SecretsStore? (b) is SettingsStore.OnboardingComplete true?
If either check fails → open SettingsWindow (US-022) BEFORE the permissions panel from US-014 and block further app initialization until SettingsWindow raises SettingsSaved or the user quits
SettingsWindow in first-run mode shows a friendly header: "Welcome to Clicky — let's set up your API keys" instead of the normal "Settings" header

After successful save, SettingsStore.OnboardingComplete is set to true and the normal app init flow continues: construct tray icon → overlay manager → CompanionManager → permissions panel (US-014) auto-opens if any OS permission is still missing
On launches where both checks pass, the normal flow runs unchanged (tray icon appears silently in background, no window opens)
If the user closes SettingsWindow via the X button (not Save, not Quit) during first-run, the app exits cleanly — never leave the app half-initialized

Runtime detection: if the user deletes their key from SecretsStore externally (or DPAPI decryption fails due to a restored backup), the next push-to-talk attempt fails gracefully by surfacing the error to CompanionViewModel as a one-shot toast-style banner reading "Your API key is missing or invalid. Open Settings to fix.", AND re-opens SettingsWindow automatically

Unit tests: first-run with no secrets opens SettingsWindow, returning user with complete secrets does not, partial config (LLM key present but AssemblyAI missing) opens SettingsWindow with a highlighted inline error on the missing field
Manual test: delete `%APPDATA%\Clicky\secrets.bin`, launch app, verify SettingsWindow opens with welcome header
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-023 - First-run setup flow
userStories.notes: The failure-mode path (key deleted or revoked mid-session) is the one most likely to cause support issues for other users. Spend time on it: make the error surfacing specific, actionable, and non-modal. Do NOT silently retry.

userStories.title: Tray Model submenu for runtime LLM switching
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between Claude and GLM from the tray menu without restarting Clicky, so I can compare providers or hot-swap if one service is down.
acceptanceCriteria: TrayIconManager's context menu gains a new `Model` submenu inserted between `Settings...` and `Quit`
The submenu contains one radio-style item per available (provider, model) pair: Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude Opus 4.6, GLM-4.6V, GLM-4.5V
The currently-active model is marked with ● (bullet) or a check-mark icon
Items whose required API key is NOT in SecretsStore are grayed out and show a tooltip: `Add your <provider> key in Settings to enable this model`

Clicking an enabled item triggers `CompanionManager.SwapLlmClientAsync(provider, model)`: (a) cancels any in-flight Claude/TTS task, (b) disposes the old ILlmClient, (c) constructs a new AnthropicDirectClient or ZaiDirectClient via the same factory App.xaml.cs uses at startup, (d) updates SettingsStore.LlmProvider + LlmModel, (e) updates CompanionViewModel.LlmModelDisplay so the panel footer reflects the change

CompanionManager.SwapLlmClientAsync is safe to call while the CompanionManager is in any VoiceState — if currently Listening/Processing/Responding, the swap waits for or cancels the current turn before taking effect, and the next push-to-talk uses the new client
ClickyAnalytics (US-016) fires a `model_switched` event with properties `{ from_provider, from_model, to_provider, to_model }` (respects the existing analyticsOptOut flag)
Unit tests: SwapLlmClientAsync transitions, grayed-out item enablement logic based on SecretsStore contents, VoiceState restoration to Idle after swap
Manual test: with a valid push-to-talk turn mid-stream, click a different model — verify the stream is cleanly cancelled and the next turn uses the new client
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-024 - Tray Model submenu for runtime LLM switching
userStories.notes: The mid-turn swap is the tricky case. Look at how US-010's CompanionManager already cancels currentResponseTask on re-press; SwapLlmClientAsync can reuse the same cancellation plumbing.

userStories.title: Shippable-product polish: README, installer notes, error copy, analytics events
userStories.description: As a prospective user finding Clicky on GitHub, I want a README that makes the value proposition clear, a setup flow I can complete without reading docs, and error messages that tell me how to fix problems rather than surfacing stack traces.
acceptanceCriteria: windows/README.md `Using Clicky` section rewritten for end-users (not developers): what it does, what keys you need, links to sign up for each service, approximate cost expectations, how to install
windows/README.md explicitly notes: `worker/` is legacy / optional shared-server mode used by the Mac reference implementation and not required for the Windows build

Top-level README.md (repo root) updated so the Windows port is presented as a first-class product, not an afterthought, with a screenshot of SettingsWindow and a screenshot of the blue cursor in action

All user-facing error copy audited: any `Exception.Message` directly surfaced to the UI must be replaced with a friendly `{what happened} + {what to do}` pair. Example: `Couldn't reach Anthropic — check your internet connection or your API key in Settings` instead of `The operation was canceled.`
CompanionViewModel exposes a new `LastError` string + clear command that the panel shows as a dismissable inline banner

ClickyAnalytics gets three new events: `onboarding_completed` (fired on first successful SettingsSaved), `settings_opened` (tray menu click), `provider_test_run` (properties: service, success, error_category). All respect analyticsOptOut

Installer script (windows/scripts/clicky-installer.iss from US-017) updated: the `[Files]` section ensures appsettings.json is NOT shipped (we no longer need WorkerBaseUrl there), uninstall section optionally offers to delete `%APPDATA%\Clicky\` contents with an explicit opt-in checkbox

Manual end-to-end test on a clean Windows 11 VM: run Setup_Clicky.exe, launch Clicky from Start Menu, complete SettingsWindow with real keys, grant permissions, perform one successful push-to-talk turn with Claude, switch to GLM via tray menu, perform one more successful turn. Document this in progress.txt
Build: 0 warnings. Tests: all pass
Commit all changes with message: feat: US-025 - Shippable polish for README installer copy analytics
userStories.notes: This is the story that distinguishes a hobby project from a product you can point friends at. Don't rush it. The copy audit is the most labor-intensive piece — grep for `throw new`, `.Message`, `MessageBox.Show`, and every user-visible string in XAML files.
